His stellar knock, coupled with Rohit Sharma's 79, guided India to a commanding nine-wicket victory and a 3-0 series whitewash.  Prasidh Krishna's five-wicket haul earlier restricted Afghanistan to 218, setting the stage for Jaiswal's record-breaking performance.

Gill praised his team's execution and highlighted the upcoming England series as crucial preparation for the 2027 World Cup.

UK PM Keir Starmer expected to announce resignation on Monday: Report
description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is reportedly poised to announce his resignation on Monday, following mounting pressure after Andy Burnham's significant by-election win.  Discussions with key figures suggest Starmer is considering a departure timetable.  While he previously vowed to fight any challenge, allies of Burnham and Wes Streeting are urging a smooth transition.  Starmer has been assessing his support within his cabinet.

description: Tax cut on low-alcohol beverages: CPI(M) alleges it benefits liquor firms, govt rejects charges

title: FSSAI targets misleading ads as brands race to sell ‘healthy’ food
description: India's food regulator, FSSAI, is cracking down on misleading health claims on food and supplement labels. Products marketed as "natural," "healthy," or "no added sugar" are under scrutiny for potentially deceiving consumers. This intensified enforcement follows rising complaints and social media attention, aiming to ensure transparency and protect public health by urging consumers to look beyond catchy marketing phrases.

title: At Lahore's 'Eton', Pakistani alum pays tribute to Indian dost
description: A classroom at Lahore's Aitchison College, once attended by leaders from undivided Punjab, now bridges India and Pakistan.  A tribute to former Punjab CM Harcharan Singh Brar, funded by his 100-year-old schoolmate Syed Babar Ali, highlights enduring friendships forged before Partition. This initiative is part of a larger effort to remember the institution's pre-1947 past, honouring students and teachers scattered by history.

title: Palantir CEO roasts Mamdani voters over their 'less valuable' Ivy League degree
description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp has publicly criticized New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, suggesting that Ivy League graduates who support him feel their education is devalued compared to practical professions. Karp also claimed that many Democrats privately express doubts about Mamdani's agenda, urging them to speak out.  The tech executive also touched upon the critical AI race and his personal sleep habits.

title: Protests mount in Bangladesh over alleged desecration of Hindu deity
description: Hindu communities across Bangladesh are protesting alleged government inaction following an incident where a picture of Lord Ram was reportedly desecrated. Leaders expressed deep concern over repeated attacks on minorities, emphasizing their rights as citizens and vowing to resist "evil forces" threatening religious harmony. Demonstrations, including human chains and rallies, demand swift legal action against perpetrators.

title: CBSE to consult key stakeholders on digital marking system
description: CBSE's on-screen marking system for Class XII exams faces an uncertain future. The board will consult students, teachers, and parents before deciding on its continuation for 2027 and potential expansion to Class X.  This review follows complaints about the system's debut, including scanning issues and unevaluated answers, and aims to address gaps exposed during recent exams and rebuild trust.

title: UK detains Indian captain of sanctioned ship, family in Uttarakhand pleads for release
description: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities are coordinating efforts for his release.

This statement contradicts Rahul Gandhi's claim that the issue was ignored.  The BJP has seized on Tharoor's remarks, highlighting the divergence within Congress and his praise for the Prime Minister's leadership.

title: Indian, Pak Army officers shake hands during a repatriation process along LoC
description: In a gesture of goodwill, the Indian Army repatriated a Pakistani national, Asad Khan, apprehended along the Line of Control in Kupwara last week. Khan, who reportedly crossed the LoC inadvertently while seeking employment, was treated with dignity and compassion. The handover at the Aman Setu peace bridge signifies a potential easing of tensions, underscoring the Indian Army's commitment to humanitarian values and professionalism.

description: Ladakh's political groups, Leh Apex Body and Kargil Democratic Alliance, have called for a shutdown on June 23, accusing the Centre of reneging on agreements regarding legislative powers and constitutional safeguards. They warn of renewed agitation if their demands for full statehood and Sixth Schedule status are not met, emphasizing that public mobilization will proceed despite the Dalai Lama's upcoming visit.

title: Kheer Bhawani yatra begins; 8,500 pilgrims in 214 buses leave Jammu
description: Over 8,500 pilgrims, predominantly Kashmiri Pandits, embarked on their journey from Jammu for the annual Kheer Bhawani Mela.  The significant increase in registrations this year is attributed to a peaceful environment, enhanced security, and comprehensive arrangements. Officials have been stationed at various shrines across Kashmir to ensure smooth proceedings for the devotees participating in the revered yatra.

"Raavana Mavanda" and "Oru Pere Varalaru" from Vijay's upcoming film, alongside TVK campaign anthems, were played, drawing criticism for a perceived lack of discipline at government events. Netizens expressed dismay, calling the administration a "joke."

title: With eyes on 2027 polls, BJP chief Nitin Nabin begins Punjab tour
description: BJP chief Nitin Nabin launched a three-day Punjab visit, emphasizing a vision for the state mirroring Maharaja Ranjit Singh's era. His tour, including prayers at the Golden Temple and a Jalandhar roadshow, signals intensified preparations for the 2027 Assembly elections. Nabin met with the Punjab BJP core committee to strategize on organizational strengthening and booth-level expansion, aiming to form a 'double-engine government' under PM Modi's leadership.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-test: Multi-layered checks in place; control room to monitor process in Gujarat
description: Medical aspirants in Gujarat are gearing up for the NEET re-exam this Sunday following a paper leak. Unprecedented security measures are in place, including GPS-tracked question paper transport, CCTV surveillance at all 211 centres, and rigorous frisking for over 79,000 students. Authorities have established a central control room and deployed senior police officials to ensure a smooth and secure examination process, aiming to prevent any malpractice.

Authorities are working to identify the caller and confirm the paper's authenticity, suspecting a fraudulent scheme ahead of the NEET re-exam.

Hum do, hamare 1.9: What India’s falling fertility rates reveal
description: India, once concerned about a population boom, now faces a declining birth rate, with the Total Fertility Rate dropping to 1.9 children per woman, below the replacement level. This significant shift reflects women's increased educational attainment, career focus, and later marriages, leading to more personal choices about family planning. The narrative is evolving from population control to individual decisions, marking a societal transition.

'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Happy Father’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to make dad feel special
description: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.
